title: atriplex lentiformis subsp. typica
intro: Atriplex lentiformis subsp. typica, also known as big sagebrush, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Atriplex lentiformis subsp. typica include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
